pr_description:
Hi @kefranabg !

I am referring Author's Github Website page URL as Author Homepage in the codebase. Please let me know if you want me to change this.

**Some points that will help during the code review:**
* Screenshot of the README
 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22813027/66192445-1b67db80-e6ae-11e9-8851-c9fae6df8d4b.png)

* I am using `node-fetch` library to make the get request. 

* I have added some tests but I guess I need to add a few more to get 100% code coverage. I will do that soon. 

* I needed to update the snapshots to make the tests pass.

Please take a look and let me know what you think. 
Thank you.

pr_description:
Referencing Issue #54 

If a package exists on NPM, it adds the dynamic NPM version badge. If it does not exist on NPM, the version badge stays as the default static version.

Changes:
- Added a new function in project-infos.js called getVersionBadgeInfo() that returns an object of the for {type: string, data: string }.
     - The type will either be "dynamic", if the package exists on NPM, and "static" if it does not. If the type is "static" the data will be the version number like before. If the type is "dynamic", the data will be the name of the package.

- In the markdown templates, the badge is set based on the version badge info.

- Updated the tests the reflect the new changes to "version" in  project-infos.

- Changed the snapshot. 

Testing:

- Used npm link and a test repo to test the badges.
     - Used a package name that existed on NPM to ensure that a dynamic badge was generated.
     - Used a  package name that did not exist on NPM to ensure that a dynamic badge was generated.

Please let me know if any of this needs to be changed.
commit_message: added dynamic version badge if package exits on NPM
